nodes.name: JWT Authentication
nodes.description: All authentication endpoints must use JWT. Do not use session cookie auth or basic auth.
nodes.directive: All authentication endpoints must use JWT. Do not use session cookie auth or basic auth.
nodes.rationale: Simplifies horizontal scaling and stateless API execution.

nodes.name: Token expiration
nodes.description: JWT tokens must have an expiration window of exactly 15 minutes.
nodes.directive: JWT tokens must have an expiration window of exactly 15 minutes.

nodes.name: Repository pattern
nodes.description: Use Repository Pattern to abstract data access layer.
nodes.rationale: Allows mocking database during unit testing.

nodes.name: Azure AD B2C Integration
nodes.description: All user authentication and identity management must be handled by Azure AD B2C. Local user databases for passwords are strictly prohibited.
nodes.directive: All user authentication and identity management must be handled by Azure AD B2C. Local user databases for passwords are strictly prohibited.
nodes.rationale: Centralizes identity management and delegates security compliance to Microsoft.

nodes.name: MSAL React Usage
nodes.description: The frontend React app must use the @azure/msal-react and @azure/msal-browser libraries for integrating with Azure AD B2C.
nodes.directive: The frontend React app must use the @azure/msal-react and @azure/msal-browser libraries for integrating with Azure AD B2C.

nodes.name: Token Validation Middleware
nodes.description: Backend services must validate the Azure AD B2C JWT token signature using the Microsoft Identity platform public keys (JWKS) before processing any protected route.
nodes.rationale: Ensures tokens are not forged and are issued by our specific B2C tenant.

nodes.name: Secure Token Storage
nodes.description: Never store JWT tokens in localStorage. Configure MSAL to use sessionStorage or secure HttpOnly cookies for token persistence.
nodes.directive: Never store JWT tokens in localStorage. Configure MSAL to use sessionStorage or secure HttpOnly cookies for token persistence.
nodes.rationale: Mitigates XSS (Cross-Site Scripting) attacks extracting access tokens.

nodes.name: Fastify API Gateway
nodes.description: All new backend services must use Fastify as the primary API Gateway due to its performance benefits. Legacy Express routes should be migrated when touched.
nodes.directive: All new backend services must use Fastify as the primary API Gateway due to its performance benefits. Legacy Express routes should be migrated when touched.
nodes.rationale: Fastify offers higher throughput and lower overhead compared to Express.

nodes.name: Express Middleware Compatibility
nodes.description: If using Express middleware in Fastify, you must use the @fastify/express plugin to ensure compatibility.
nodes.directive: If using Express middleware in Fastify, you must use the @fastify/express plugin to ensure compatibility.

nodes.name: Thin Controllers
nodes.description: Controllers should only handle HTTP request/response mapping and validation. All business logic must reside in the Service layer.
nodes.rationale: Promotes separation of concerns and testability.

nodes.name: Asynchronous Error Handling
nodes.description: Do not use try/catch in every route handler. Use a centralized error handling middleware to catch unhandled promise rejections.
nodes.directive: Do not use try/catch in every route handler. Use a centralized error handling middleware to catch unhandled promise rejections.
nodes.rationale: Prevents memory leaks and ensures consistent error responses.

nodes.name: Functional Components
nodes.description: All React components must be written as Functional Components using Hooks. Class components are strictly prohibited.
nodes.directive: All React components must be written as Functional Components using Hooks. Class components are strictly prohibited.
nodes.rationale: Hooks provide better logic reuse and simpler mental models.

nodes.name: Custom Hooks for Data Fetching
nodes.description: Abstract all API calls and data fetching logic into custom hooks (e.g., useUser(), useAuth()) rather than fetching directly inside UI components.
nodes.structure: UI Component -> Custom Hook -> API Service
nodes.rationale: Separates presentation from data fetching logic.

nodes.name: State Management
nodes.description: Global state should be managed via React Context for low-frequency updates, and Zustand for high-frequency updates. Redux is deprecated.
nodes.directive: Global state should be managed via React Context for low-frequency updates, and Zustand for high-frequency updates. Redux is deprecated.

nodes.name: Strict TypeScript Interfaces
nodes.description: Every component must define a strict Props interface. Do not use any or Record<string, unknown> for props.
nodes.directive: Every component must define a strict Props interface. Do not use any or Record<string, unknown> for props.
nodes.rationale: Catches runtime errors at compile time and improves IDE autocompletion.
